tokens: Okay, Davy, when you get to (Surface) 8-6 on water recharge, I've got a change for you.
When you can take it, just let me know.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: You two guys really know how to impress people, I'll tell you.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Well, you know, we sure have a lot of good things going for us.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Are y'all ready to go ahead with this battery management?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Now, I know where you are in the checklist.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We're trying to get to lunch time here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, the sooner you do it, the sooner you get to sleep, and we'll be very anxiously awaiting that.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Dick, I missed your comment when we lost comm there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, I gave you a go-ahead on that battery; and, concerning your lunch, we're anxiously awaiting you to do all that so you can get to bed.
We've got a time problem, as you might well recognize.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: And, we'll talk to you a little bit about that when you get a chance.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: That's a...I'm sorry, I got the wrong one, Dick.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we didn't have you on high bit rate, but we'll get it as soon as you do.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Okay, we are looking at your batteries now, they look great.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Okay, Jim, we're getting good high bit rate and we'll watch those batteries for a little while.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: (Long Pause) Houston, ED (Explosive Device) batteries both checked at 37.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I have a weight report for you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: SRC was 40 (terrestrial pounds); bag 3 was 30; bag 6 [was] 33 - for a total of 103.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And Houston, Hadley Base again, standing by for your cue to go PCM, Low, and Power Amp, Off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Say, Jim, just give us a couple more minutes.
(Pause) I'll call you back on it.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Okay, we're through looking at your batteries.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Jim and Dave, can you let me know where-abouts you are.
I've got some word for you before you get to PLSS recharge and the ETB stowage
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Jim's just getting out of his suit now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Look, on that telemetry biomed, since we looked at you last night, you can leave that where it is in the "Right" (LMP) position, and we'll look at Jim tonight.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: And I'll tell you the secret to living up here is getting out of these suits.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I believe you're absolutely right.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Okay, and I owe you a word on your PLSS recharge (on surface 8-6).
When you have those things in that good vertical position, they want you to charge that descent water for 10 minutes instead of 5 you have on your checklist.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: The only additional thing I have for you, and you can just listen to this.
Before you put the film away in the ETB (as per Surface 8-7), I've got a little word on the 16 millimeter stuff, and also I'll talk to you about Jim's 70 millimeter when you come back to me.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We'll get back to you when we get to that point.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I just thought I'd let you know that you've worked right through your eat period.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We were just looking at that.
As a matter of fact, I think we've got ten minutes left, don't we?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, I'm sorry about that, old friend.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We just went by it, didn't we?
Have to skip some of the things up here, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Ready to copy about the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, can you hold up until we clear up the comm, please?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: (Static clears momentarily) Dave, this is Houston.
How do you hear me now?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: (Static clears) Okay, it seems to have cleared.
Dave, on your ETB loading, if you've got page 8-7 there, for the ETB, I've got just a couple of changes.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Okay, I've got it right in front of me with a pencil, so go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; on the black-and-white column (means "line") that has "VV" and "WW", add "Roger-Roger."
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: You're down quite a bit (in volume).
Scratch the black-and-white magazine "Mike-Mike" that was on the 500, and leave that on-board the LM.
And you can use WW, which is on the next line up there, for the 500 millimeter.
Add two more 16-millimeters: Golf-Golf and Hotel-Hotel.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: So far I've added Roger, scratched Mike, and added Golf and Hotel on the 16s.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And you can use WW on the five hundred.
On the sixteen-hundred (means 16) millimeter stuff, some instructions that never got up to you and didn't get to us until today.
On those 16-millimeter mags, the limit on advancing that film is one frame.

And there are two red perforations...(correcting himself) two red marks alongside the window there; and, if you can line up the perforation hole with those two red marks, we ought to be in business on the 16 millimeter.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We knew about that, primarily because of the split-frame problem, and we've made sure all along that those perforations were lined up with the red marks...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...and we did that with the mags today, Too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, that's very good; and we've done all we can with the 16.

And I don't know what you've done with Jimmy's 70-millimeter, but the only thing we can suggest from down here (is), if the Mag still has exposures left, play the game with the two white windows on the camera and the magazine; and if that mag doesn't work, try to find one that does, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Okay; we haven't had a chance to exercise it yet, but when we get through (garbled) we'll give you a call.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We just wanted to remind you of it.
Would you verify the MESA heater circuit-breaker's open for me?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Okay; it was closed, and now it's open.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I guess you still have taping procedures for your PLSS, and we'll call you when the VHF window comes up with Al (Worden in the Command Module).
Further, there will be no science debriefing for you guys tonight.
You did such a superb job today that we don't have very many questions down here.
And we would like to use that time to get you gone, and get you to bed.

As you can well guess, we're holding lift-off time right where it is, and we want you to get a good night's sleep, so we're going to have to steal some time from somewhere and we're doing the best we can.
We're looking at the EVA for tomorrow for you.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We know it's in good hands and we'll be standing by.
We'll give you a call when we get around to those PLSS recharges.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And you want to talk to Al this pass?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Yeah, let's say hello to our old Endeavour buddy up there, huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Roger; Al's going to give you a call on simplex Alpha about now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You must be on the other side of the mountains and we'll just stand by until you get over it, because you are always broken over there on the other side.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (No response) How do you read me now, Falcon?
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: You're a little broken too, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Okay, we'll wait until you get closer overhead, so you get past the mountains.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'm just about overhead now.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: How are things going up there?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, getting lots and lots of good data.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Got a little over 100 pounds (of rocks) today.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Got up the side of the mountain.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Pretty spectacular up beside that mountain, I bet.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Oh, man, it was super, just super.
We got some great pictures for you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I hope I got some good ones for you, too.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Yeah, I tell you, I hope you can see these Rover tracks, because outside the LM here, it looks like a freeway.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, you can collect you another bunch of rocks tomorrow and bring them home.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Okay, make a nice little place for them.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, we'll make a place for whatever you bring home.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Hey, Al, throw my soap down, will you?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Don't mind if I use it, do you?
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Well, I haven't had a chance to use it yet, but I might tonight.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: I suggest you wait until tomorrow night, Al (too faint).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I guess it'll pay for us all to do that tomorrow night.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Yeah, I tell you, our suits were pretty (too faint) yesterday, but (too faint).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, the Rover is doing absolutely super.
We were going up the side of that mountain like (too faint).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, it's really a super little machine.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (No answer) Falcon, can you read Endeavour now?
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Roger, we've seen water usage in the last few minutes; can you confirm that this is a PLSS refill?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're 9 minutes into the 10 minutes refill.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we have two other items for you here.
I have some lift-off times, when Jim is ready to copy.
And I'd like to reinforce for your consideration Dick's comments on sleep time.
We have a hard limit down here of 7 hours from the time you crawl into the
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think we're making some time up.
We've got one PLSS just now finishing it's recharge, and the suits are all stowed and we've stowed some of the rocks, so we're in pretty good shape.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, and the sooner you can call me to say if you're in the
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And I guess we're a little gun shy (because of the leak discovered at the end of EVA-1), but we're still seeing some water usage, and we'd like to confirm that you're getting some good out of it, and it's not running out on the floor.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And we're charging the second PLSS right now, and we're about 5 minutes into it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Say again, Dave; you're kind of weak.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I said, we're glad you're watching; and we're charging the second PLSS now, and we're about 5 minutes into it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Houston, this is Hadley Base, ready to copy lift-off pad.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Ready to copy lift-off times for T-38 through 41.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Good readback, Jim; and I think that's all I have on my list here.
We'd like a crew status report sometime before you go to bed, but unless you guys have a question, that's about all I have here on the ground.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, our PLSS recharge is about complete.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Long Pause) Houston, this is Hadley Base.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Jim, this is Bob; go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, I was wondering whether you'd heard from the Endeavour that's sailing through the Northwest Passage.
It should be at Point Barrow now.
He said he would be trying to give us a call.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Or sometime while we're on the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We've heard nothing that I know of.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Somehow he was going to try and come through Mission Control down there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, I haven't seen anything while I've been in here; and, Jim, you and Dave might think also that...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, your transmission was cut out.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It turns out we do indeed have said message, which reads: "We carry out our separate voyages in the spirit of Endeavour which unites people in their efforts to overcome the common tasks when confronted by the elements of sea and wind or harsh environment of space.
Hopefully, this force will bring the nations of Earth closer together as we explore beyond our planet in the name of 'Endeavour'." And so forth.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Thank you for the message, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, and another message, that you guys might pause to consider, is that the Surgeon is going to want you to change your sponges and tapes in the morning.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We'll be standing by for a call when you go to sleep.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Beautiful, and we'd like to verify a couple of things before you go to bed; one, that you called up the computer and then put it back to sleep; and two, that you've changed the lithium hydroxide canisters.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And do you have any crew status?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No medication and in the sack.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, and that was a super day today, Dave and Jim.
We'll wake you up in approximately 7 hours.
transcript.speaker: Parker
